Imports

hast_common, idents_types, pragmas, hast_common

Types

ObjectBranch[N] = ref object
  declNode*: Option[N]
  pragma*: Option[Pragma[N]]
  flds*: seq[ObjectField[N]] ## Fields in the case branch
  docComment*: string
  codeComment*: string
  case isElse*: bool         ## Whether this branch is placed under `else` in
                             ## case object.
  of true:
      notOfValue*: seq[N]

  of false:
      ofValue*: seq[N]       ## Match value for case branch
Single branch of case object 
ObjectField[N] = ref object
  declNode*: Option[N]
  iinfo*: LineInfo
  docComment*: string
  codeComment*: string ## More complex representation of object's field - supports
                       ## recursive fields with case objects.
  pragma*: Option[Pragma[N]]
  value*: Option[N]
  isExported*: bool
  case isTuple*: bool
  of true:
      tupleIdx*: int

  of false:
      name*: string

  fldType*: NType[N]         ## Type of field value
  isChecked*: bool
  case isKind*: bool
  of true:
      selected*: int         ## Index of selected branch
      branches*: seq[ObjectBranch[N]] ## List of all
                                      ## branches as `value-branch` pairs.
    
  of false:
      nil

  
ObjectDecl[N] = ref object
  iinfo*: LineInfo
  docComment*: string
  codeComment*: string
  exported*: bool
  pragma*: Option[Pragma[N]]
  name*: NType[N]            ## Name for an object
  flds*: seq[ObjectField[N]]
  declNode*: Option[N]
  base*: Option[N]

ObjectPathElem[N] = object
  kindField*: ObjectField[N]
  case isElse*: bool
  of true:
      notOfValue*: seq[N]

  of false:
      ofValue*: seq[N]

  
ObjectPath[N] = seq[ObjectPathElem[N]]
NObjectBranch = ObjectBranch[NimNode]
NObjectPathElem = ObjectPathElem[NimNode]
NObjectField = ObjectField[NimNode]
NObjectDecl = ObjectDecl[NimNode]
NObjectPath = seq[NObjectPathElem]
PObjectDecl = ObjectDecl[PNode]
PObjectField = ObjectField[PNode]
PObjectBranch = ObjectBranch[PNode]
ObjKind = enum
  okConstant,               ## Literal value
  okSequence,               ## Sequence of items
  okTable, ## List of key-value pairs with single types for keys and
            ## values
  okComposed ## Named list of field-value pairs with possilby
             ## different types for fields (and values). List name is optional
             ## (unnamed object), field name is optional (unnamed fields)
ObjAccessor = object
  case kind*: ObjKind
  of okConstant:
      nil

  of okSequence:
      idx*: int

  of okComposed:
      name*: string

  of okTable:
      key*: string

  
ObjPath = seq[ObjAccessor]
ObjTree = object
  ignored*: bool
  path*: ObjPath             ## Path of object in original tree
  objId*: int                ## Unique object id
  isPrimitive*: bool         ## Whether or not value can be considered primitive
  annotation*: string        ## String annotation for object
  styling* {...}{.requiresinit.}: PrintStyling ## Print styling for object
  case kind*: ObjKind
  of okConstant:
      constType*: string     ## Type of the value
      strlit*: string        ## Value representation in string form
    
  of okSequence:
      itemType*: string      ## Type of the sequence item
      valItems*: seq[ObjTree] ## List of values
    
  of okTable:
      keyStyling* {...}{.requiresinit.}: PrintStyling
      keyType*: string       ## Type of table key
      valType*: string       ## TYpe of value key
      valPairs*: seq[tuple[key: string, val: ObjTree]] ## List of
                                                       ## key-value pairs for table
    
  of okComposed:
      namedObject*: bool     ## This object's type has a name? (tuples
                             ## does not have name for a tyep)
      namedFields*: bool     ## Fields have dedicated names? (anonymous
                             ## tuple does not have a name for fields)
      name*: string          ## Name for an object
      fldPairs*: seq[tuple[name: string, value: ObjTree]] ## Sequence
                                                          ## of field-value pairs for object representation

Fields

isPrimitive: Value is primitve or not?

Primitive value will be added to graphviz node export as part of the table (in regular export) as oppposed to non-primitive value (it will be rendered as separate node). By default primitive values are int, string, float etc. types, tuples/objects that are (1) composed only from primitive types ((int, int)), (2) have four fields or less. Also tables/sequences with four elements or less are considered primitive if (1) both keys and values are primitive (2) container has four elements or less.

ObjElem[Conf] = object
  case isValue: bool
  of true:
      text*: string
      config*: Conf

  of false:
      relType*: ObjRelationKind
      targetId*: int

  
ValField = ObjectField[ObjTree]
ValFieldBranch = ObjectBranch[ObjTree]

Procs

proc newPObjectDecl(name: string;
                    flds: seq[tuple[name: string, ftype: NType[PNode]]] = @[];
                    exported: bool = true; pragma: PPragma = PPragma();
                    docComment: string = ""; codeComment: string = "";
                    genParams: seq[NType[PNode]] = @[];
                    iinfo: LineInfo = defaultIInfo): PObjectDecl {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
proc newCall(obj: NObjectDecl): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
proc isFinalBranch[N](field: ObjectField[N]): bool
proc addBranchBody[N](main: var N; branch: ObjectBranch[N]; body: N)
proc newObjectPathElem[N](field: ObjectField[N]; branch: ObjectBranch[N]): ObjectPathElem[
    N]
proc getFlatFieldsPath[N](decl: ObjectDecl[N]): seq[
    tuple[field: ObjectField[N], path: ObjectPath[N]]]
proc newDot[N](self: N; field: ObjectField[N]): N
proc newDot[N](self: string; field: ObjectField[N]): N
proc newDot[N](self: N; path: seq[ObjectField[N]]; useLast: bool = true): N
proc eachFieldMut[N](branch: var ObjectBranch[N];
                     cb: proc (field: var ObjectField[N])): void
Execute callback on each field in mutable object branch, recursively. 
proc eachFieldMut[N](obj: var ObjectDecl[N];
                     cb: proc (field: var ObjectField[N])): void
Execute callback on each field in mutable object, recursively. 
proc eachField[N](branch: ObjectBranch[N]; cb: proc (field: ObjectField[N])): void
Execute callback on each field in branch, recursively 
proc eachField[N](obj: ObjectDecl[N]; cb: proc (field: ObjectField[N])): void
Execute callback on each field in object, recurisively. 
proc eachCase(fld: NObjectField; objId: NimNode;
              cb: proc (field: NObjectField): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
proc eachCase(objId: NimNode; obj: NObjectDecl;
              cb: proc (field: NObjectField): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
Recursively generate case statement for object. objid is and identifier for object - case statement will use <objid>.<fldId>. obj is a description for structure. Callback cb will be executed on all fields - both isKind or not. 
proc eachParallelCase(fld: NObjectField; objId: (NimNode, NimNode);
                      cb: proc (field: NObjectField): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc eachParallelCase(objid: (NimNode, NimNode); obj: NObjectDecl;
                      cb: proc (field: NObjectField): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
Generate parallel case statement for two objects in objid. Run callback on each field. Generated case statement will have form if lhs.fld == rhs.fld: case lhs.fld 
proc eachStaticPath(kind: NimNode; obj: NObjectDecl;
                    cb: proc (fields: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError], tags: [].}
proc eachPath(fld: NObjectField; self: NimNode; parent: NObjectPath;
              cb: proc (path: NObjectPath; fields: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    deprecated: "Use `mapPath` insetad", raises: [], tags: [].}
Deprecated: Use `mapPath` insetad 
proc eachPath(self: NimNode; obj: NObjectDecl;
              cb: proc (path: NObjectPath; fields: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    deprecated, raises: [], tags: [].}
Deprecated 
proc eachPath(self: NimNode; obj: NObjectDecl;
              cb: proc (fields: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    deprecated, raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
Deprecated 
proc prettyPrintConverter(val: PNode; path: seq[int] = @[0]): ObjTree {...}{.
    raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect].}
proc mapBranches(field: NObjectField; parent: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]];
                 caseExpr: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]): NimNode;
    mapBranch: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]; branch: NObjectBranch): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapBranches(obj: NObjectDecl;
                 caseExpr: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]): NimNode;
    mapBranch: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]; branch: NObjectBranch): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapCase(field: NObjectField; parent: seq[NObjectField]; caseExpr: proc (
    field: NObjectField; path: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode;
             cb: proc (field: NObjectField; path: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapCase(objectDecl: NObjectDecl; caseExpr: proc (field: NObjectField;
    path: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode;
             cb: proc (field: NObjectField; path: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapGroups(field: NObjectField; parent: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]];
               caseExpr: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]): NimNode;
    mapGroup: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]; group: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapGroups(obj: NObjectDecl;
               caseExpr: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]): NimNode;
    mapGroup: proc (path: seq[ObjectField[NimNode]]; group: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapPath(fld: NObjectField; parentField: seq[NObjectField];
             parentPath: NObjectPath;
             caseExpr: proc (path: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode;
             cb: proc (path: NObjectPath; fields: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
proc mapPath(obj: NObjectDecl;
             caseExpr: proc (path: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode;
             cb: proc (path: NObjectPath; fields: seq[NObjectField]): NimNode): NimNode {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
Visit each group of fields in object described by obj and generate case statement with all possible object paths. Arguments for callback
  • NObjectPath is a sequence of kind field values that must be active in order for execution to reach this path in case statement.

Second argument is a list of fields that can be accessed at that path.

Funcs

func newObjectField[N](name: string; cxtype: NType[N]; docComment: string = "";
                       codeComment: string = ""; exported: bool = true;
                       value: Option[N] = none(N)): ObjectField[N]
func addField[N](obj: var ObjectDecl[N]; name: string; cxtype: NType[N];
                 docComment: string = ""; codeComment: string = "";
                 exported: bool = true) {...}{.
    deprecated: "use .addField(newObjectField())".}
Deprecated: use .addField(newObjectField()) 
func addField[N](obj: var ObjectDecl[N]; field: ObjectField[N])
func newObjectCaseField[N](name: string; fieldType: NType[N];
                           docComment: string = ""; codeComment: string = "";
                           exported: bool = true): ObjectField[N]
func newObjectOfBranch[N](ofValue: N): ObjectBranch[N]
func newObjectOfBranch[N](ofValue: seq[N]): ObjectBranch[N]
func newObjectElseBranch[N](): ObjectBranch[N]
func addField[N](branch: var ObjectBranch[N]; field: ObjectField[N])
func addBranch[N](field: var ObjectField[N]; branch: ObjectBranch[N])
func addBranch[N](field: var ObjectField[N]; ofValue: N | seq[N];
                  fields: varargs[ObjectField[N]])
func isMarkedWith(fld: NObjectField; str: string): bool {...}{.
    raises: [ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
func isMarkedWith(fld: NObjectField; pragma, arg: string): bool {...}{.
    raises: [ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
func isSkipField(fld: NObjectField; arg: string): bool {...}{.
    raises: [ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
func isReqInit(fld: NObjectField): bool {...}{.raises: [ImplementKindError], tags: [].}
func onPath(self: NimNode; path: NObjectPath): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
Generate check for object self to check if it is currently on path. 
func toNNode[N](branch: ObjectBranch[N]): N
func toNNode[N](fld: ObjectField[N]; standalone: bool = false): N
func toExported[N](ntype: NType[N]; exported: bool): tuple[head, genparams: N]
func toNNode[N](obj: ObjectDecl[N]; standalone: bool = false): N
func toNimNode(obj: NObjectDecl): NimNode {...}{.raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError],
    tags: [].}
func fields[N](objectDecl: ObjectDecl[N]): seq[ObjectField[N]]
func newOType(name: string; gparams: seq[string] = @[]): NType[ObjTree] {...}{.
    raises: [], tags: [].}
func `==`(lhs, rhs: ObjTree): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func `==`[Node](lhs, rhs: ObjectField[Node]): bool
func makeObjElem[Conf](text: string; conf: Conf): ObjElem[Conf]
func initObjTree(): ObjTree {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func annotate(tree: var ObjTree; annotation: string): void {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
func stylize(tree: var ObjTree; conf: PrintStyling): void {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func styleTerm(str: string; conf: PrintStyling): string {...}{.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [].}
func seqAccs(idx: int): ObjAccessor {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func objAccs(name: string): ObjAccessor {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func tableAccs(name: string): ObjAccessor {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
func getAtPath(obj: var ObjTree; path: ObjPath): var ObjTree {...}{.
    raises: [ArgumentError, ValueError, ImplementError], tags: [].}
func hasPragma[N](decl: ObjectDecl[N]; name: string): bool
func getPragmaArgs[N](decl: ObjectDecl[N]; name: string): seq[N]
func eachPragmaMut[N](branch: var ObjectBranch[N];
                      cb: proc (opt: var Option[Pragma[N]]))
Execute callback on each annotation in mutable branch, recursively - all fields in all branches are visited. 
func eachPragmaMut[N](obj: var ObjectDecl[N];
                      cb: proc (pragma: var Option[Pragma[N]]))
Execute callback on each annotation in mutable object, recurisively - all fields and subfields are visited. Callback runs on both kind and non-kind fields. Annotation is not guaranteed to be some, and it might be possible for callback to make it none (removing unnecessary annotations for example) 
func eachPragma[N](branch: ObjectBranch[N]; cb: proc (pragma: Option[Pragma[N]]))
func eachPragma[N](obj: ObjectDecl[N]; cb: proc (opt: Option[Pragma[N]]))

Templates

template mapItKindFields(branch: NObjectBranch; body: untyped): untyped
template mapItBranches(obj: NObjectDecl; expr, body: untyped): untyped
template mapItCase(objectDecl: NObjectDecl; expr, body: untyped): untyped
template mapItGroups(objectDecl: NObjectDecl; expr, body: untyped): untyped
template mapItKindFields(objectDecl: NObjectDecl; expr, body): untyped
template mapItPlainFields(objectDecl: NObjectDecl; expr, body): untyped
template mapItPath(objectDecl: NObjectDecl; expr, body: untyped): untyped
Convenience template for [[code:mapPath()]]
  • @arg{expr} :: case builder expressions, [[code:mapPath().caseExpr]]
  • @arg{body} :: group body converter expression, [[code:mapPath().cb]]

Made with Nim. Generated: 2021-07-13 18:34:57 UTC
